document.text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Marcus Hollingsworth        MRN: 4029-887-A
DOB: 03/14/1958                       Age: 67
Admitted: 04/11/2026                  Discharged: 04/22/2026
Attending: Dr. Priya Subramanian, MD
Service: Cardiology, Duke Regional Hospital

HPI: Mr. Hollingsworth is a 67-year-old male with a history of CAD s/p
PCI 2019, HTN, and HLD who presented to the ED on April 11 with substernal
chest pain radiating to his left jaw. Wife (Carla, 919-555-2014) accompanied
him. Troponin peaked at 1.84.

Hospital course: Underwent cardiac cath on hospital day 1 with drug-eluting
stent to the mid-LAD. No further events. Tolerated aspirin/clopidogrel
without bleeding. Echo on 4/19 showed EF 45-50%.

Discharge meds: ASA 81mg daily, clopidogrel 75mg daily, atorvastatin 80mg
qHS, metoprolol succinate 50mg daily, lisinopril 10mg daily.

Follow-up: Cardiology with Dr. Subramanian on 05/06/2026 at 10:30 AM,
clinic at 4002 Erwin Rd, Durham NC 27710. PCP Dr. Allen Chen at Triangle
Family Medicine within 1 week. Patient may be reached at (919) 555-7821 or
m.hollingsworth@gmail.com.

HISTORY & PHYSICAL

Patient: Aaliyah Okonkwo-Bright           Date: 04/29/2026 0815
DOB: 11/02/1991                           MRN: 778-3201-99
Admitting MD: Dr. Hae-Won Park
Location: UNC Hospitals, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill NC

CC: 34F G2P1 at 38+4 weeks presenting in active labor.

HPI: Patient began having regular contractions at 0430 this morning, q4
minutes. SROM at 0610. Husband Devon drove her in from their home in
Hillsborough. Prenatal care with Dr. Mei Liu at Carrboro OBGYN, last visit
4/24/26 unremarkable.

PMH: Mild gestational diabetes (diet-controlled), seasonal allergies.
PSH: Cesarean 2019 for breech (son Jaylen, now 6).
Meds: PNV daily, metformin held since labor onset.
Allergies: Sulfa (rash).

Social: Lives with husband at 412 Cornwallis St, Hillsborough NC 27278.
Works as elementary school teacher (Orange County Schools). Cell:
984-555-0162. Emergency contact: mother, Folake Okonkwo, +234-803-555-1190
(currently in Lagos).

Exam: VS 124/78, HR 92, T 98.6. Cervix 5 cm / 80% / 0 station.

Plan: Admit L&D bed 7, anesthesia consult for epidural, GBS+ — start
penicillin per protocol, continuous fetal monitoring.

EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT NOTE

Patient: Travis Pemberton           Acct: V20260428-7741
DOB: 08/22/1979                      MRN: 1180224
Arrival: 04/28/2026 23:14            Mode: Private vehicle
Provider: Dr. Reema Anand, ED attending

CC: Right-sided abdominal pain x 6 hours.

HPI: 46M presents with sudden-onset RLQ pain that began around 1700 today
while at work (Pemberton Auto Body, 2210 Capital Blvd, Raleigh). Pain rated
8/10, sharp, associated with one episode of nonbilious emesis. No fevers
documented at home, but felt warm. Last meal lunch at noon — Bojangles.
Drove himself to Wake Med Raleigh Campus when pain worsened.

PMH: Obesity (BMI 34), prediabetes. No prior surgeries.
Meds: None.
Social: Lives alone, divorced. One adult daughter (Brittany, contacted at
919-555-3360 per pt request). Smokes 1/2 ppd, drinks weekends.

Exam: T 100.4, HR 108, BP 142/88. Tender RLQ with rebound, +Rovsing.
Labs: WBC 16.2, lactate 1.4, lipase WNL.
Imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast read by Dr. P. Goldfarb at 00:42 —
acute appendicitis with periappendiceal stranding, no perf.

Disposition: Surgery consult to Dr. Hannah Nguyen, OR booked 02:30. NPO,
IV ceftriaxone + metronidazole, 1L LR. Daughter notified.

OPERATIVE NOTE

Patient: Eleanor Whitfield-Sato       MRN: 90234-Q
DOB: 12/05/1953                        Date of surgery: 04/27/2026
Surgeon: Dr. Bo Tanaka                 Assistant: Dr. Rashida Khalil, PGY-3
Anesthesia: Dr. Vincent Mauro (general endotracheal)
Facility: Mercy Medical Center, OR 4

Pre-op diagnosis: Symptomatic cholelithiasis.
Post-op diagnosis: Same.
Procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy with intraoperative cholangiogram.

Indications: 72F with 4-month history of post-prandial RUQ pain. Ultrasound
on 03/18/2026 showed multiple gallstones, no ductal dilation. Discussed
with patient and her son Marcus (POA, contact 415-555-9087) at clinic
visit 4/14.

Findings: Inflamed gallbladder with multiple stones; cholangiogram normal.
Specimens: Gallbladder to pathology (specimen ID PA26-04-8821).
EBL: <10 mL.  Fluids: 1100 mL LR.  UOP: 250 mL.
Complications: None.

Disposition: PACU then short-stay unit, expected DC tomorrow morning.
Husband Hiroshi to drive home to 88 Magnolia Ct, Pittsburgh PA 15217.
Follow-up in clinic with Dr. Tanaka in 2 weeks. Office: 412-555-2230.

DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — HOSPITAL DAY 4

Patient: Dmitri Voloshyn                  MRN: 553-209-X
DOB: 06/30/1972                            Room: 7N-412
Attending: Dr. Sandra Olufemi              Service: General medicine
Date: April 29, 2026

Subjective: Pt reports feeling "much better" today. No chest pain, no
SOB at rest. Slept 5 hours, woke for 2g vanco at 0400. Wife Iryna visited
yesterday evening; she will pick him up on discharge to their home at
2204 Birchcrest Lane, Apt 3B, Cleveland OH 44109.

Objective: Afebrile x48h. T 98.2, BP 118/72, HR 78, SpO2 96% RA.
Labs (4/29 AM): WBC 9.1 (down from 18.4 on admission), Cr 1.0, K 4.1.
Blood cultures from 4/26 NGTD x72h.
Micro: MSSA from initial cx, sensitive to nafcillin — switched 4/27.

Assessment/Plan:
1) MSSA bacteremia, source likely cellulitis right lower extremity —
   continuing nafcillin 2g IV q4h, day 5 of planned 14-day course.
   ID consult Dr. Wojciech Pasternak agrees with current regimen.
2) HTN — controlled on amlodipine.
3) Disposition — case management (Lila Sanderson, ext 4421) arranging
   home infusion with PICC. Anticipate DC tomorrow if stable. Daughter
   Olena (cell 216-555-7702) will be primary contact for home health.

Pt and family agreeable. Spent 35 minutes at bedside.

— S. Olufemi, MD

NURSING SHIFT NOTE — 1900-0700

Pt Brianna Castellanos-Mwangi (MRN 882-4471), 28F admitted 4/27/26 for
DKA, now on insulin gtt at 4 units/hr titrating per protocol. AOx3 at
shift start, cooperative.

2030 — BG 312, K 3.9. Continued gtt, started D5½NS at 125/hr per Dr.
Kavanaugh's order. Mother Ruth (call from 305-555-1148) called twice
asking about transfer to step-down; updated her, will call back when bed
assigned.

2245 — Pt c/o nausea, given Zofran 4mg IV. Boyfriend Anil arrived and
stayed until 2330. Pt's address per face sheet: 990 SW 12th Ave Apt 7,
Miami FL 33135.

0145 — BG 188, transitioned to subcutaneous regimen per endocrine
(Dr. Yelena Strakhov). D/c'd insulin gtt at 0220 after 2-hour overlap.
Pt slept from 0245-0500.

0530 — Voided 400mL clear yellow. AM labs drawn, sent to lab. BG 142.
Pt asked about her work — Aldi store #2218, manager Brett Halverson,
work cell 305-555-7714. She wants discharge paperwork stating she was
admitted so she can excuse her shifts.

Plan for day shift: Endo to round, expect transfer to 5-South.

Sara Pemberton, RN BSN — Reg# NC-RN-228814

DIAGNOSTIC IMAGING — FINAL REPORT

Patient name: Henrik Salvesen-Park       Patient ID: IMG-2026-04-77129
DOB: 01/19/1965                           Sex: M
Exam: MRI Brain w/ and w/o contrast       Accession: A26104772
Performed: 04/28/2026 14:32 at Mass General, 55 Fruit Street, Boston MA
Ordering provider: Dr. Sigrid Lindqvist (Neurology, oncall pager 617-555-9911)

CLINICAL HISTORY: 61M with 3-week history of progressive left-hand weakness
and aphasia. Outpatient evaluation referred by PCP Dr. Tomas Ribeiro on
4/22/26.

COMPARISON: CT head from 04/18/2026 at Cambridge Health Alliance (limited
images obtained from outside courier).

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain pre- and
post-administration of 10 mL Gadavist IV.

FINDINGS: 3.4 x 2.8 x 3.1 cm heterogeneously enhancing mass centered in
the right frontal lobe, crossing the genu of the corpus callosum.
Surrounding vasogenic edema with mass effect on the right lateral
ventricle, 4 mm midline shift to the left. Restricted diffusion within
the enhancing rim.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right frontal mass with imaging characteristics most consistent with
   high-grade glioma. Differential includes metastasis or lymphoma.
2. Recommend neurosurgical and neuro-oncology consultation. Findings
   discussed with Dr. Lindqvist by phone at 15:08 on 04/28/2026.

Electronically signed: Pranav R. Iyer, MD — Neuroradiology
Report finalized: 04/28/2026 16:42

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Yolanda Kasprzak-Owen           Specimen: SP-26-018824
DOB: 09/03/1968                            Date received: 04/26/2026 09:14
Sex: F                                     MRN: 33-009-7741
Submitting MD: Dr. Reuben Akinwumi (general surgery)
Facility: St. Vincent's Hospital, 7700 Forest Ave, Birmingham AL 35223

Clinical history: 57F with 2.1 cm left breast mass detected on screening
mammogram 03/02/2026; biopsy positive for invasive ductal carcinoma. Now
s/p left lumpectomy with sentinel node biopsy on 04/26/2026.

Specimens received:
A. Left breast lumpectomy (oriented with sutures per Dr. Akinwumi)
B. Sentinel lymph node #1
C. Sentinel lymph node #2

Gross description: A. 4.2 x 3.8 x 2.6 cm fibrofatty tissue with single
suture marking superior margin. On sectioning, ill-defined firm tan-white
mass measuring 1.9 cm. B. 1.4 x 1.0 x 0.7 cm tan-pink lymph node, bisected.
C. 0.9 x 0.7 x 0.5 cm tan-pink lymph node.

Microscopic / final diagnosis:
A. Left breast, lumpectomy: Invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2,
   1.9 cm. Margins negative; closest 4 mm (deep). LVI not identified.
   ER 95% positive, PR 80% positive, HER2 1+ (negative).
B. Sentinel node #1: 1 of 1 negative for metastasis (0/1).
C. Sentinel node #2: 1 of 1 negative for metastasis (0/1).

Pathologic stage: pT1c pN0.

Patient/oncology contact: Tumor board scheduled 05/05/2026 1700, Conf Rm
B-2. Med onc Dr. Halima Brennan to follow. Patient cell 205-555-3389,
preferred email yolandak.owen@outlook.com.

Pathologist: Dr. Stefan Korhonen, MD, PhD — Electronic signature on file
Reported: 04/29/2026 11:08

CARDIOLOGY CONSULT NOTE

Patient: Joaquin Espinoza-Ferré         MRN: 4488120-CA
DOB: 07/14/1944                          Age: 81
Date: 04/29/2026
Consulting MD: Dr. Aiyana Standing-Bear, Cardiology
Requested by: Dr. Owen Pritchard (Hospitalist)

Reason for consult: New atrial fibrillation with RVR.

HPI: Mr. Espinoza is an 81-year-old retired postal worker admitted
yesterday from Sunset Manor Assisted Living (1820 Glenwood Ave, Apt 14,
Sacramento CA) with pneumonia. Today developed new-onset AF with rates
130-150s. Granddaughter and POA Lucia Ferré-Diaz (cell 916-555-2204) was
notified by the floor nurse.

Past cardiac hx: HTN x 30 years, mild LV hypertrophy on echo 2024.
Never had documented arrhythmia previously.

Meds on admission: Lisinopril 20mg, atorvastatin 40mg, ASA 81mg,
multivitamin. Per his daughter Mariana (called from Mexico City,
+52-55-5544-9921), patient also takes herbal teas.

Exam: 81M alert, oriented to person and place. Irregularly irregular pulse
~140. Lungs with right basilar crackles. No edema.

ECG: AF, rate 138, no acute ischemic changes.
Echo (today, bedside): EF preserved, LA dilated 4.8 cm.

Assessment/Plan:
1) New AF likely precipitated by acute infection. Rate control with IV
   diltiazem 10mg now, transition to PO 30mg q6h. Goal HR <110 at rest.
2) CHA2DS2-VASc = 4. Recommend apixaban 5mg BID once renal function
   confirmed stable. Discussed risk/benefit with patient and Lucia by
   phone at 11:20.
3) Treat underlying pneumonia per primary team.
4) Outpatient cardiology follow-up with me — Capital Heart, 2825 Capitol
   Ave, Sacramento. Front desk 916-555-7700, ext 2 for scheduling.

Thank you for the kind consultation.

— A. Standing-Bear, MD, FACC

TELEHEALTH VISIT NOTE

Patient: Sasha Petrov-Tanaka         DOB: 02/26/1996
Visit type: Video (Zoom for Healthcare)   Date/time: 04/29/2026 16:00 PT
MRN: TH-2026-554120                   Insurance: Cigna PPO, ID W22884310-01
Provider: Dr. Hannah Wexler-Goldstein, family medicine
Patient location at time of visit: Home — 4470 Telegraph Ave Apt 12,
Oakland CA 94609

CC: Medication refill, anxiety follow-up.

Subjective: Patient is a 30 y/o nonbinary software engineer at Stripe
(work email s.petrov@stripe.com) following up 6 weeks after starting
sertraline 50 mg daily. Reports good response — "the constant edge is
gone." Sleeping 7-8 hours, working from home, recently engaged to partner
Marcus Tanaka.

No SI/HI. No medication side effects beyond mild initial nausea (resolved
week 2). Therapy weekly with Dr. Olu Ade-Williams (referral from prior
visit). Family hx anxiety (mother Anya, treated for GAD).

Objective (telehealth limited): Appears well, well-groomed, eye contact
appropriate via video. Speech normal rate and rhythm. Mood "good," affect
congruent. PHQ-9 administered: 4. GAD-7: 5.

Assessment:
1. GAD — improving on sertraline 50 mg.
2. Health maintenance — due for annual labs.

Plan:
- Continue sertraline 50 mg daily; refill x 90 days sent to Walgreens
  #4488 (5450 Broadway, Oakland).
- Follow up in 3 months or sooner PRN. Pt cell 510-555-9982.
- Lab orders sent to Quest #2018 for fasting CMP, lipid panel, A1C.
- After-visit summary emailed to s.petrov.tanaka@gmail.com.

Visit duration: 22 minutes. Patient consented to telehealth and recording
disclaimer per California law.

— H. Wexler-Goldstein, MD

INTER-HOSPITAL TRANSFER SUMMARY

From: Bertie County Memorial Hospital, Windsor NC (252-555-0100)
To:   ECU Health Medical Center, Greenville NC — accepting MD Dr. Felipe
      Manríquez-Costa, trauma surgery (pager 252-555-9408)
Patient: Caleb Whitaker         DOB: 10/11/2002        MRN at sending: BCM-220198
Transfer date/time: 04/29/2026 02:47          Mode: Vidant ground EMS unit 47

Reason for transfer: Polytrauma after MVC requiring trauma center capability.

Brief history: 23-year-old male, restrained driver in single-vehicle MVC
into a tree on Hwy 17 at approximately 2310 on 04/28/2026. GCS 13 on EMS
arrival, brought to BCM as nearest facility. EMS run report #EMS-26-12044.
Family notified — mother Tracy Whitaker (252-555-7711) en route from
Roanoke Rapids; father deceased.

Injuries identified at sending facility:
- Right pneumothorax with chest tube placed by Dr. Nina Albright at 00:18
- Grade II splenic laceration (CT abdomen 23:55, read by Dr. Hossein
  Yazdani via teleradiology)
- Closed right tib/fib fracture, splinted
- Multiple facial lacerations, repaired

Vitals at transfer: BP 112/70, HR 96, RR 18, SpO2 97% on 2L NC, GCS 14.
IV access: Right AC 18g, left forearm 20g.
Lines/tubes: Right chest tube #28 to wall suction (-20), Foley.
Active issues: Tachycardia trending down, hemoglobin stable at 11.4
(initial 12.8). 1U PRBC infused at 01:30.

Meds given: Fentanyl 50 mcg x2, ondansetron 4mg, ceftriaxone 1g,
TDaP 0.5mL.

Records and imaging burned to disc, accompanying patient. Phone report
given to Dr. Manríquez-Costa at 02:31. Sending MD: Dr. Patricia Akinola.
Patient and mother both verbally consented to transfer.

PEDIATRIC CLINIC VISIT — WELL-CHILD

Patient: Amara Balogun-Reilly       DOB: 04/15/2022        Age: 4 yrs 14 days
MRN: PED-2026-008812                 Visit date: 04/29/2026 09:30
Provider: Dr. Ingrid Sjöström, MD (pediatrics)
Clinic: Riverside Pediatrics, 220 W 86th St Suite 4B, New York NY 10024

Accompanied by: Mother (Chiamaka Balogun-Reilly, cell 646-555-2718,
email c.balogun@nyu.edu — works as a researcher at NYU). Father Sean
Reilly was unable to attend.

Interval history: 4-year well-child visit. Per mom, Amara is doing well
in pre-K at Riverside Montessori. Eats varied diet, sleeps 11 hours
overnight in her own bed at home (apartment 8C). No concerns from teacher
Ms. Hartwell. Met all developmental milestones. No recent illnesses other
than a brief URI in February.

Older brother Tunde (age 7, MRN PED-2024-007711) had strep throat last
week — Amara has not had symptoms.

Vaccines: Up to date. Today receiving DTaP #5, IPV #4, MMR #2, varicella
#2 per CDC schedule. VIS sheets provided (English).

Vitals: Wt 16.8 kg (50%ile), Ht 103 cm (60%ile), BMI 15.8, HR 102,
BP 92/58 (manual, right arm), T 98.0.
Vision: 20/30 R, 20/25 L (Snellen with cards).
Hearing: Passed bilaterally.

Exam: Well-appearing, cooperative. HEENT, cardiac, lung, abdominal,
neuro, MSK exams all within normal limits. Tympanic membranes clear.

Assessment/Plan:
1. Healthy 4-year-old. Anticipatory guidance discussed (booster seat
   until 4'9", screen time, oral health — recommend dentist by age 5).
2. Vaccines given as above. Adverse reaction info reviewed.
3. Next visit: 5-year well-child in 12 months. Schedule with front desk.
4. After-visit summary printed; mom prefers paper copy.

Visit time: 25 minutes face-to-face.
— I. Sjöström, MD

PSYCHIATRY INTAKE — INITIAL EVALUATION

Patient: Rowan McAllister-Kim        DOB: 09/12/1988        Age: 37
MRN: PSY-26-441207                    Date: April 29, 2026
Provider: Dr. Benedikt Hoffmann-Krause, MD
Setting: Outpatient — Cedar Grove Behavioral Health, 1455 Pine Hollow
         Drive, Suite 210, Asheville NC 28803

Source: Self-referral via PCP Dr. Marisol Ortega-Vega; PHQ-9 of 18 at
last PCP visit on 04/02/2026.

CC (patient's words): "I haven't felt like myself since the divorce. I
need help."

HPI: 37-year-old transmasculine patient (he/him), works as line cook at
The Black Bear Tavern (downtown Asheville). Reports 4 months of
worsening low mood, anhedonia, sleep disturbance (early-morning awakening
0330-0400), 12-lb weight loss, and passive SI without plan or intent.
Onset followed separation from spouse (Jordan Kim, divorce finalized
02/14/2026). Patient now lives alone at 88 Magnolia Trace Apt B,
Asheville NC 28806.

Past psych hx: Depression episode in college (2008-2010) treated with
fluoxetine, partial response. No prior hospitalizations, no prior
suicide attempts. No SUD history, drinks 2-3 beers on weekends only.

Family hx: Mother Linda McAllister with depression (lives in Knoxville,
865-555-1148, listed as emergency contact with patient permission).
Maternal uncle completed suicide at age 52.

Social: Estranged from biological father. Close friend Devika (last name
unknown to patient — "Dev from work") is primary support. T currently on
testosterone cypionate 100mg q week prescribed by Dr. Henrietta Park at
Tranquil Endocrine since 2019.

Mental status: Casually dressed, fair grooming. Cooperative. Speech
slowed. Mood "tired." Affect blunted, congruent. TC linear. No SI today.
No psychosis. Insight and judgment intact.

Assessment: Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate-to-severe,
without psychotic features. PHQ-9 today: 19.

Plan:
- Start sertraline 25 mg daily x 7 days, then 50 mg daily.
- Weekly therapy with Lena Brewer, LCSW (intake call 828-555-9012).
- Safety plan reviewed and documented; 988 reviewed.
- Follow up with me in 3 weeks (05/20/2026 at 14:00). Reachable at
  828-555-3340 or rmckim@protonmail.com.
- Coordination of care note faxed to Dr. Ortega-Vega 828-555-7700.

Visit duration: 60 minutes (intake CPT 90792).
— B. Hoffmann-Krause, MD

PATIENT RESULT LETTER

April 29, 2026

Maeve Kowalski-Antonelli
6622 Sycamore Lane
Royal Oak, MI 48073

Re: Lab results from your visit on April 22, 2026
MRN: 88-220-4419

Dear Ms. Kowalski-Antonelli,

I am writing to share the results of the bloodwork we drew at your annual
physical last week.

Your overall results look good. Your cholesterol panel is improved
compared to last year — total cholesterol 188 (down from 217), LDL 102,
HDL 58, triglycerides 140. Your hemoglobin A1C is 5.4 (normal). Liver
and kidney function are within normal range.

Two values are slightly outside the normal range and worth following:

  - Vitamin D 25-OH: 22 ng/mL (low; goal >30). I recommend starting
    over-the-counter vitamin D3 2000 IU daily. We can recheck in 3 months.

  - TSH: 4.9 mIU/L (mildly elevated; normal 0.4-4.0). This may be a
    one-time finding. Please come in for a repeat thyroid panel in
    6 weeks. You can call our scheduling line at (248) 555-2200 or use
    the patient portal.

If you have questions, you can message me through the portal or call
the office. Your appointment for the repeat thyroid panel can be brief —
just a blood draw, no provider visit needed unless you'd like one.

Best wishes,

Mira Singh-Bhattacharya, MD
Beacon Family Medicine
1230 Woodward Heights, Suite 4
Hazel Park, MI 48030
office (248) 555-2200 | fax (248) 555-2201

MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP

Patient: Bartholomew Onyekwere III    MRN: ONC-7728-Q
DOB: 05/22/1959                        Visit date: 04/29/2026 14:00
Provider: Dr. Liesl van der Berg, MD, FACP
Cancer center: Hope Cancer Institute, 4400 Belmont Park N, Nashville TN

Diagnosis: Stage IIIB non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 19
deletion, diagnosed 11/14/2024. Currently on osimertinib 80mg daily,
month 18 of treatment.

Interval history: Patient is here with wife Adeline (cell 615-555-1840)
and son Bartholomew Jr. (a physician, internal medicine, who has been
helping coordinate care; bart.onyekwere.jr@vumc.org). Last imaging
03/18/2026 — continued partial response, primary lesion 1.4 cm (down
from 1.6 cm prior). No new symptoms. Energy good — golfed 9 holes at
Belle Meade twice this month. Dry skin and minor paronychia of the right
thumb, managed conservatively per nail care handout.

Side effect review: Diarrhea — 0. Rash — minimal, controlled with
moisturizer. Cardiac — no chest pain, no edema. Last echo 02/2026,
LVEF 60%.

Vitals: Wt 78.4 kg (stable). BP 132/78. HR 70. SpO2 97% RA.

Labs today (drawn 13:32, accession LB-26-44819):
  CBC: WBC 5.8, Hgb 13.4, Plt 224 — normal.
  CMP: Cr 0.9, AST 24, ALT 28 — normal.

Assessment/Plan:
1) NSCLC on osimertinib, sustained partial response — continue current
   dose. No dose modification needed.
2) Repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast in 8 weeks; will order
   now for 06/24/2026 at our imaging center (615-555-7720).
3) Cardiac surveillance — schedule echo in 6 months.
4) Ongoing supportive care — refilled emollient prescription to CVS at
   2200 West End Ave, Nashville (Rx #88-1024-A).
5) Patient continues to do well. He plans a trip to Lagos to visit his
   brother (Chukwuemeka, age 71) in late June; cleared for travel,
   reviewed medication supply (will bring extra month).

Patient cell: 615-555-3382. Email: bart.onyekwere@gmail.com. Pt portal
preferred for non-urgent messages.

Next visit: 06/24/2026 with imaging same day.
— L. van der Berg, MD
